objective: Expand the Security offline reference E2E worker write-set by exactly the private governance and validation-support files required for the domain full suite to accept two new E2E tools.
executable_blocker: After the worker adds two private E2E tools, the domain active-core doctor requires tool classification and two validation tests need to remain robust to the higher tool count and ignored local Crypto KB artifacts.
preservation_contract: the original E2E adapter and registry source behavior remains bounded to the two new private source files
active-core inventory changes classify only the two new E2E tools and their direct consumers
core-doctor test keeps fail-closed no-unclassified coverage while allowing the exact discovered tool count to grow
Crypto KB smoke test remains a real-or-clean-checkout gate and ignores only incomplete ignored local database artifacts
no public Bridge source contract schema ledger dependency workflow or root configuration file is changed by the worker
no private Security program path target evidence report payload dataset secret account material or scientific truth enters the public repository
no live target scan exploit phishing brute force publication deployment UI or authority escalation is introduced
acceptance_commands: private focused adapter and E2E tests with PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 and no pytest cache
private core-doctor plus clean-checkout Crypto KB smoke regression
private full repo suite against exact pinned public Bridge source
exact seven-file write-set privacy dependency network live-authority and weakening scans
stop_conditions: write-set expansion beyond the original four E2E files plus exactly the three added validation-support files is required
active-core inventory classifies unrelated tools or changes primary command ownership
Crypto KB smoke accepts an incomplete real inventory as real
private full suite remains red after the amended worker is applied
rollback: Agent 0 rejects the amended worker handoff or reverts the seven-file private worker commit. The public Bridge repository, private owner checkout, existing domain registries and external systems remain unchanged.
